lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: How are you getting on with helping my dad?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did you say he lived?

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: Dad lives in the south-east of Varrock, west of Aubury's Rune Shop. His name's Yarlo. Old Man Yarlo, they call him. He's sure to be at home, so keep looking around.

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: Hello - it's lovely to see a Mahogany Homes contractor on their rounds! Now, can I help you with something? Or might you be able to do me a little favour?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does this Mahogany Homes company do?

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: Oh, we redecorate people's houses, fixing things and building new furniture. Mending cabinets, building new chairs and tables - things like that.

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: When a client hires us to redecorate their house, we send a builder round to do the job, and we ensure the builder's paid for their work.

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: We can't hire you for that kind of work now, but perhaps you could do me a little favour instead, if you're willing. I can pay!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of favour do you want me to do?

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: Well, you see, it's about my dad. His house is really decrepit. I need someone to rip out the broken furniture and build him some better stuff. I'll pay you if you do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you in a construction company yourself though?

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: See, my dad just - um - found the house. No-one was using it, so he just settled in. It's all perfectly harmless, and he's a sick old man anyway, so...

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: Basically, he's not really meant to be there. And we'd prefer that the landlord not find out, so we're keeping this job off the record. I'll pay you myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why has he got such a bad house?

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: Some kind of economic downturn wiped out his pension plans. It happens more often than you'd think. But I'd rather not talk about it. Can you help him, though?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the job.

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: Nothing too hard. You'll have to rip out the broken furniture, then build replacements. Dad will talk you through it - he's a retired carpenter himself. He loves talking about construction.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me where he lives, and I'll do the job.

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: Fabulous! Dad lives in the south-east of Varrock, west of Aubury's Rune Shop. His name's Yarlo. Old Man Yarlo, they call him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd rather not.

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: Okay, suit yourself.

sections.title: Talking to Old Man Yarlo again if none of the old furniture was removed

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: Ah, young man, how's the redecoration coming on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What am I supposed to be doing again?

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: You'll want to start by removing all the tatty old furniture. There are two stools, two tables, a chair and a rotten carpet to remove, as well as the campbed. Let me know when you've done that.

sections.title: Talking to Old Man Yarlo again if some of the old furniture was removed

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: Ah, young man, how's the redecoration coming on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been removing the old furniture.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: You still need to remove: - A broken stool in the kitchen - A broken stool in the bedroom - A broken chair in the bedroom

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: - A broken table in the kitchen - A broken table in the bedroom - The old campbed - The rotten carpet

sections.title: Talking to Old Man Yarlo again if all the old furniture was removed

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: Ah, young man, how's the redecoration coming on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've finished removing all the old furniture.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: Jolly good, young man. Now, we'll need to have a talk about what you'll build for me to replace it.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: You see, I used to be a carpenter myself, before the arthritis got me. Such wonderful things I could build. Let me tell you about the things Construction can do for you...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this necessary? I've got a house of my own, in [city], and I've been learning about Construction for a while.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: Now, I was just getting round to telling you about the furniture I want you to build for me. For a start, I'd like two new stools, two new tables and a new chair.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: Now, for the bed, I've got something special in mind. This town's quite damp, and I don't want the moisture rising up the bed into my blankets. But I've found some wood that resists water.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: I think that's everything - two stools, two tables, a chair, a bed and a carpet. Off you trot, young man. I'll be here if you have any questions.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: You young people are always rushing around trying to optimise the fun out of life. There are other ways to enjoy the world, you know! But I'm sure you know best.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: Now, I was just getting round to telling you about the furniture I want you to build for me. For a start, I'd like two new stools, two new tables and a new chair.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: What brings you here, young man?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Marlo sent me to fix your furniture.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: My boy Marlo sent you? Oh yes, you've come to help with my furniture. It's in a terrible state. You'll want to start by removing all the tatty old furniture.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: There are two stools, two tables, a chair and a rotten carpet to replace. I need a better bed too. Let me know when you've removed all the old furniture.

sections.title: Talking to Old Man Yarlo again

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: Do you need some help with the building work, young man?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I build furniture?

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: Ah. Look in the place where the new furniture will go...

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: You'll need a hammer and saw. I can supply those if you need them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I get planks, nails and cloth?

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: From the sawmill! They sell nails and cloth at reasonable prices. You'd have to take your own logs there, though, to get planks, but there are plenty of trees to chop nearby.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: Watch out, though - nails can bend, if you're not careful. You should probably get some spares, just in case.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: You've got a hammer and a saw. I wouldn't be able to offer you anything else.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some tools.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: Would you like a [hammer/saw]? I can spare one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the waxwood for the bed.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: This town's quite damp, and I don't want the moisture rising up the bed into my blankets. But I've found some wood that resists water.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get on with it.

sections.title: Talking to the sawmill operator at the Lumberyard
Talking to any other sawmill operator

lines.speaker: Sawmill Operator
lines.text: Hello there. Do you want me to make some planks for you? I can make planks from wood, oak, teak and mahogany logs. Or would you like to buy some other housing supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some waxwood planks for Old Man Yarlo.

lines.speaker: Sawmill Operator
lines.text: I don't know any Old Man Yarlo, and I'd rather not work with that horrible material.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But he said he knew the chap at the sawmill, and you'd make waxwood planks for him.

lines.speaker: Sawmill Operator
lines.text: Perhaps he was thinking of some other sawmill operator.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: Do you want me to make some planks for you? I can make planks from wood, oak, teak and mahogany logs. Or would you like to buy some other housing supplies?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need some waxwood planks for Old Man Yarlo.

lines.speaker: Sawmill operator
lines.text: That old geezer? Oh, alright. I won't charge for this, since it's for him.

sections.title: Talking to Old Man Yarlo after building everything
Talking to Old Man Yarlo before claiming reward from Marlo

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: Hello again, young man. Has my boy Marlo rewarded you yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll get round to visiting him soon.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: Yes, you should do that. You've earned it.

lines.speaker: Old Man Yarlo
lines.text: That's everything! Thank you so much, young man. Trot off back to my boy Marlo, now. He'll have something for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll head back to Marlo.

sections.title: Talking to Marlo after building everything for his dad

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've finished building the new furniture for your dad.

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: That's wonderful news! Dad's going to be so much happier. I'm very grateful! Now, I promised to pay you for your work...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, what have you got for me?

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: Now, I can't spare very much money, but I've got some supplies in this crate that might help with your own Construction work.

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: I've included some handy spell tablets that'll take you to your house, to cut down on the walking a bit, as well as one for visiting my boss in Falador. Now, let me tell you some tricks of the trade too...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I do some other kind of building work for you?

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: I'm so sorry, but you're not qualified to do contracts for Mahogany Homes yet. Just keep helping my dad, for now, and perhaps I can sort that out for you.

sections.title: Talking to Marlo again but not receiving the reward and not having a house

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: I haven't forgotten that I promised to pay you for your help. Is this a good time for that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, what have you got for me?

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: Now, I can't spare very much money, but I've persuaded the Estate Agent to let me give you a house of your own. You'll be able to decorate it, like you did for my dad's house.

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: Normally a new house costs 1,000 coins, so that's a bit of a saving. Also, I've got a crate of supplies to help you decorate it.

lines.speaker: Marlo
lines.text: I've included some handy spell tablets that'll take you to your house, to cut down on the walking a bit, as well as one for visiting my boss in Falador. Let me tell you some tricks of the trade too...
